authors_affiliation: Marwen Sallem, Amina Ghrissi, Adnen Saadaoui, Vicente Zarzoso; National Institute of Applied Sciences and Technology, MMA Laboratory (Tunisia); Université Côte d'Azur, CNRS, I3S Laboratory (France); Vneuron (Tunisia)

self_reported_metrics.other: Challenge validation score S=0.368 (hidden validation set); accuracy 0.54 is validation accuracy of the retained model. Preliminary (unofficial phase) 5-fold CV training scores: F2-score=0.77, G2-score=0.58, GM=0.67 (zero padding); F2=0.74, G2=0.54, GM=0.63 (signal repetition). Preliminary test set: F2-score=0.77, G2-score=0.55, GM=0.65.

model.architecture_details: VGG-ECG: a 13-layer fully convolutional 1D CNN inspired by VGG16, composed of blocks of 2-3 successive Conv3 (1x3 receptive field) layers alternated with average pooling layers; channel widths progress 64,64 / 128,128 / 256,256 (x4 blocks); dropout (p=0.3) for regularization.
model.key_novelty: Adapts the VGG16 image-classification architecture to 1D multichannel ECG by using a deep stack of small-receptive-field (1x3) convolutions with average pooling, combined with truncation/zero-padding and downsampling strategies to standardize variable-length, multi-source recordings.

input.signal_length_window: First 3000 time samples after downsampling kept (recordings originally 10s to 30min); shorter signals zero-padded or repeated to fill length. Preliminary experiments used the first 18000 samples of padded recordings.
input.sampling_rate: Downsampled to 100 Hz (benchmarked against 50 Hz and 150 Hz; original recordings had varying sampling frequencies depending on data source)
input.preprocessing: Downsampling to a standard frequency (100 Hz found best), truncation to first 3000 samples, zero padding or signal repetition for length standardization. Low-pass filtering and standard normalization were tested but did not improve scores.

data.training_datasets: All official Challenge 2020 training data: 43101 12-lead ECG recordings from four data sources, filtered down to 37794 recordings belonging to at least one of the 27 scored classes; no external data used.
data.class_imbalance_handling: Class weights applied to neural network nodes to balance classes during training.
data.augmentation_techniques: not reported (proposed as future work, not implemented in this study)

training.loss_function: Binary cross-entropy (chosen over mean squared error, which performed worse)
training.cross_validation_strategy: 5-fold cross-validation used in preliminary experiments; final model trained on a 3/4 train / 1/4 validation split of the dataset
training.hardware: Google Cloud Platform, n1-standard-4 instance (4 vCPUs, 15GB memory), NVIDIA Tesla T4 GPU; implemented in Python 3.7 / TensorFlow 2.3, tracked with Weights & Biases (Wandb)

contribution.summary: Proposes VGG-ECG, a deep 1D CNN inspired by VGG16, for multilabel 12-lead ECG rhythm classification, and addresses variable-length, multi-source recordings via signal truncation/padding and downsampling to a common sampling rate.
